opportunities.rationale: Shopee parent crashed ~24% on 3 Mar after Q4 missed EPS despite revenue beat; heavy investment spend spooked markets. RSI hit ~27 (deeply oversold). Revenue +36% YoY and EBITDA +33% YoY intact; selloff is sentiment-driven. Require stabilisation above 87 before entry; stop below crash-day low.

MongoDB crashed ~27% on 3 Mar: Q4 FY26 beat (EPS $1.65 vs $1.48, rev +27% YoY) was eclipsed by softer FY27 guidance. Barclays kept Overweight ($370 PT), Needham maintained Buy ($300). Stock stabilised near $270 in deeply oversold territory. Entry on hold above 260; invalidated below 245.

BlackRock fell >10% by 6 Mar after Bloomberg reported the firm capped withdrawals from its $26B HPS Corporate Lending Fund, sparking a sector-wide private credit selloff. Underlying AUM and fee revenue remain solid; the withdrawal cap is a technical fund management action. Conviction low: private credit sentiment remains fragile and FOMC (18-19 Mar) adds macro uncertainty. Size small; require stabilisation above 950.

CoreWeave dropped ~18.5% in late Feb on weak margin guidance and large 2026 capex. Revenue backlog remains strong and AI cloud infrastructure demand is structural. Conviction downgraded to low: no clean stabilisation base yet and elevated VIX reduces speculative rebound probability. Only enter on confirmed bounce above 78 with volume.

BioNTech fell ~17% on 10 Mar after Q4 earnings guided to lower Covid-19 vaccine revenue, overshadowing a sizeable oncology and mRNA pipeline. The single-session gap-down into deeply oversold territory (~RSI 28) fits the panic-stabilisation rebound pattern. Fundamental balance sheet remains strong; selloff is revenue-mix sentiment-driven. Citi PT $130. Require stabilisation above 77 before entry; invalidated below 70.

Amazon dropped ~9% in early Feb after guiding for ~$200B of 2026 capex, far above prior expectations, despite strong Q4 revenue and re-accelerating AWS. Since the selloff, stock has partially recovered but remains below pre-report levels. Capex shock and AI-spending fatigue drove the move, not demand deterioration. AWS re-acceleration and e-commerce strength provide fundamental support for mean-reversion back toward 238 resistance. Invalidated on daily close below 200.
